extra.reason: Added AF external and embedded-libraries entry to .pkgmeta
Updated JetTools.toc: SavedVariablesPerCharacter → SavedVariables, added AF toc to load order
Core.lua rewritten: profile data model, full profile API, settings routed through active profile, live-reload on profile switch, AF.RegisterAddon call

Options.lua fully rewritten with AF-based UI: headered frame, left sidebar with ButtonGroup radio selection, profile dropdown/new/delete strip, right ScrollFrame with per-module pane populated from GetOptions() schema using AF widgets
Options.lua fully rewritten with pure WoW API widgets (no AbstractFramework). RequiredDeps removed from .toc, required-dependencies removed from .pkgmeta, AF reference removed from Core.lua.
CombatRes tracker module implemented in Modules/CombatRes.lua
Combat tab groups CombatStatus/CombatTimer/CombatRes; Reminders tab groups PetReminders/StealthReminder

Replaced FontString arrow (U+25BE, missing from WoW fonts) with a Texture using Interface\Buttons\Arrow-Down-Up + SetTexCoord to crop the down-arrow half. No font glyph dependency, always renders correctly.

Hoisted _groupState to module-level local 'groupState' before BuildOptionControl definition. Replaced all BuildOptionControl._groupState references with groupState. Lua does not allow indexing function values.

Fixed by introducing isPreviewMode flag in both CombatTimer and CombatRes. The flag is set true in ShowPreview() and false in HidePreview(). When active: Disable() skips hiding the frame, UpdateDisplay() skips its hide-when-disabled guard, and OnUpdate skips its normal rendering path. OnSettingChanged now checks isPreviewMode directly instead of querying the global options frame state.

Fixed by overriding all four UIPanelButtonTemplate texture slots (normal, pushed, highlight, disabled) in a new JT_StripButtonTextures() helper. Applied to JT_CreateButton for all styles, the dropdown button, and sidebar nav buttons. Blizzard red/orange artwork can no longer bleed through in any interactive state.
Fixed Options.lua OnShow to check settings.enabled before calling ShowPreview(). Disabled modules no longer show their preview overlay when the options panel opens.

Fixed ApplyPosition() in both CombatTimer and CombatRes with a new ResolveAnchorFrame() function that: (1) supports case-insensitive friendly aliases (chat, minimap, playerframe, targetframe, etc.), (2) validates the resolved object has GetObjectType, (3) prints a warning and falls back to UIParent on invalid input, (4) temporarily clears SetClampedToScreen before SetPoint so large X/Y offsets aren't silently clipped.
